about.title: About the Asia Rocsta
about.lead: Korean off-roader built by Asia Motors (a KIA subsidiary) between 1990 and 1999, derived from the KIA KM410 military vehicle and powered by Mazda mechanics. A simple, rugged, easy-to-repair 4x4 — exactly what this archive is here to document.
about.facts.bodiesValue: SWB 3-door / LWB 5-door · soft or hard top
about.engine.f8.notes: SOHC 8v, carburetor, same block as the Mazda 626 (GC/GD) and B1800. Easy to service, known for burning oil after ~150,000 km.
about.engine.r2.notes: SOHC 8v naturally aspirated, derived from the Mazda B2200 / E2200 / Bongo pickups. Critical timing belt every 80,000 km.
about.tl.1990: Asia Rocsta launches in South Korea, based on the KM410 military chassis.
about.tl.1993: Arrives in Spain imported by Aresa Motor; becomes an accessible alternative to the Suzuki Samurai.
about.tl.1994: Minor mechanical update and equipment improvements.
about.tl.1997: The Asian financial crisis hits Asia Motors hard.
about.tl.1999: KIA fully absorbs the brand; Rocsta production ends.
about.tl.2020: Rise of the restorer community in Spain, Italy and Korea.
about.mission.title: Why this archive exists
about.mission.desc: The Rocsta is a rare vehicle with no official spares network and scattered documentation. This project gathers parts, cross-references, guides and common faults in one open place so any owner can keep their vehicle alive.
